id.description: The unique ID of the new inference endpoint.

arn.description: The ARN for the new inference endpoint.

statusCode.description: The HTTP response code: 200 if the delete was successful, or 204 if there were no statistics to delete.

statisticsId.description: The ID of the statistics generation run that is currently occurring.

statusCode.description: The HTTP response code: 200 if the delete was successful, or 204 if there were no statistics to delete.

statisticsId.description: The ID of the statistics generation run that is currently occurring.

status.description: Set to healthy if the instance is not experiencing problems. If the instance is recovering from a crash or from being rebooted and there are active transactions running from the latest server shutdown, status is set to recovery.

startTime.description: Set to the UTC time at which the current server process started.

dbEngineVersion.description: Set to the Neptune engine version running on your DB cluster. If this engine version has been manually patched since it was released, the version number is prefixed by Patch-.

role.description: Set to reader if the instance is a read-replica, or to writer if the instance is the primary instance.

dfeQueryEngine.description: Set to enabled if the DFE engine is fully enabled, or to viaQueryHint (the default) if the DFE engine is only used with queries that have the useDFE query hint set to true.

gremlin.description: Contains information about the Gremlin query language available on your cluster. Specifically, it contains a version field that specifies the current TinkerPop version being used by the engine.

sparql.description: Contains information about the SPARQL query language available on your cluster. Specifically, it contains a version field that specifies the current SPARQL version being used by the engine.

opencypher.description: Contains information about the openCypher query language available on your cluster. Specifically, it contains a version field that specifies the current operCypher version being used by the engine.

labMode.description: Contains Lab Mode settings being used by the engine.

rollingBackTrxCount.description: If there are transactions being rolled back, this field is set to the number of such transactions. If there are none, the field doesn't appear at all.

rollingBackTrxEarliestStartTime.description: Set to the start time of the earliest transaction being rolled back. If no transactions are being rolled back, the field doesn't appear at all.

features.description: Contains status information about the features enabled on your DB cluster.

settings.description: Contains information about the current settings on your DB cluster. For example, contains the current cluster query timeout setting (clusterQueryTimeoutInMs).

status.description: The HTTP return code of the request. If the request succeeded, the code is 200. See Common error codes for DFE statistics request for a list of common errors.

autoCompute.description: Indicates whether or not automatic statistics generation is enabled.

active.description: Indicates whether or not DFE statistics generation is enabled at all.

statisticsId.description: Reports the ID of the current statistics generation run. A value of -1 indicates that no statistics have been generated.

date.description: The UTC time at which DFE statistics have most recently been generated.

note.description: A note about problems in the case where statistics are invalid.

signatureInfo.description:
A StatisticsSummary structure that contains:

  • signatureCount - The total number of signatures across all characteristic sets.

  • instanceCount - The total number of characteristic-set instances.

  • predicateCount - The total number of unique predicates.

lastEventId.description:
Sequence identifier of the last change in the stream response.

An event ID is composed of two fields: a commitNum, which identifies a transaction that changed the graph, and an opNum, which identifies a specific operation within that transaction:

lastTrxTimestampInMillis.description: The time at which the commit for the transaction was requested, in milliseconds from the Unix epoch.

format.description: Serialization format for the change records being returned. Currently, the only supported value is PG_JSON.

records.description: An array of serialized change-log stream records included in the response.

totalRecords.description: The total number of records in the response.

commitTimestampInMillis.description: The time at which the commit for the transaction was requested, in milliseconds from the Unix epoch.

eventId.description: The sequence identifier of the stream change record.

data.description: The serialized Gremlin or openCypher change record.

isLastOp.description: Only present if this operation is the last one in its transaction. If present, it is set to true. It is useful for ensuring that an entire transaction is consumed.

statusCode.description: The HTTP return code of the request. If the request succeeded, the code is 200.

payload.description: Payload containing the property graph summary response.

version.description: The version of this graph summary response.

lastStatisticsComputationTime.description: The timestamp, in ISO 8601 format, of the time at which Neptune last computed statistics.

statusCode.description: The HTTP return code of the request. If the request succeeded, the code is 200.

payload.description: Payload for an RDF graph summary response

version.description: The version of this graph summary response.

lastStatisticsComputationTime.description: The timestamp, in ISO 8601 format, of the time at which Neptune last computed statistics.

graphSummary.description: The graph summary of an RDF graph. See Graph summary response for an RDF graph.

status.description:
The HTTP return code of the request. If the request succeeded, the code is 200. See Common error codes for DFE statistics request for a list of common errors.

When invoking this operation in a Neptune cluster that has IAM authentication enabled, the IAM user or role making the request must have a policy attached that allows the neptune-db:GetStatisticsStatus IAM action in that cluster.

autoCompute.description: Indicates whether or not automatic statistics generation is enabled.

active.description: Indicates whether or not DFE statistics generation is enabled at all.

statisticsId.description: Reports the ID of the current statistics generation run. A value of -1 indicates that no statistics have been generated.

date.description: The UTC time at which DFE statistics have most recently been generated.

note.description: A note about problems in the case where statistics are invalid.

signatureInfo.description:
A StatisticsSummary structure that contains:

  • signatureCount - The total number of signatures across all characteristic sets.

  • instanceCount - The total number of characteristic-set instances.

  • predicateCount - The total number of unique predicates.

lastEventId.description:
Sequence identifier of the last change in the stream response.

An event ID is composed of two fields: a commitNum, which identifies a transaction that changed the graph, and an opNum, which identifies a specific operation within that transaction:

lastTrxTimestampInMillis.description: The time at which the commit for the transaction was requested, in milliseconds from the Unix epoch.

format.description: Serialization format for the change records being returned. Currently, the only supported value is NQUADS.

records.description: An array of serialized change-log stream records included in the response.

totalRecords.description: The total number of records in the response.

commitTimestampInMillis.description: The time at which the commit for the transaction was requested, in milliseconds from the Unix epoch.

eventId.description: The sequence identifier of the stream change record.

data.description: The serialized SPARQL change record. The serialization formats of each record are described in more detail in Serialization Formats in Neptune Streams.

isLastOp.description: Only present if this operation is the last one in its transaction. If present, it is set to true. It is useful for ensuring that an entire transaction is consumed.

acceptedQueryCount.description: The number of queries that have been accepted but not yet completed, including queries in the queue.

runningQueryCount.description: The number of Gremlin queries currently running.

queryString.description: The query string of the Gremlin query.

queryEvalStats.description: The query statistics of the Gremlin query.

status.description: Returns the status of the job list request.

ids.description: A page listing data processing job IDs.
A page from the list of inference endpoint IDs.
A page of the list of model training job IDs.
A page from the list of model transform IDs.

acceptedQueryCount.description: The number of queries that have been accepted but not yet completed, including queries in the queue.

runningQueryCount.description: The number of currently running openCypher queries.

queryString.description: The query string of the Gremlin query.

queryEvalStats.description: The query statistics of the Gremlin query.

status.description: The HTTP return code of the request. If the request succeeded, the code is 200.

payload.description: This is only returned for refresh mode.

statisticsId.description: The ID of the statistics generation run that is currently occurring.

status.description: The HTTP return code of the request. If the request succeeded, the code is 200.

payload.description: This is only returned for refresh mode.

statisticsId.description: The ID of the statistics generation run that is currently occurring.

status.description: The cancellation status of the openCypher query.

payload.description: The cancelation payload for the openCypher query.

status.description: The status is only returned for the performDatabaseReset action, and indicates whether or not the fast reset rquest is accepted.

payload.description: The payload is only returned by the initiateDatabaseReset action, and contains the unique token to use with the performDatabaseReset action to make the reset occur.

token.description: A UUID generated by the database in the initiateDatabaseReset action, and then consumed by the performDatabaseReset to reset the database.

output.description: A text blob containing the Gremlin explain result, as described in Tuning Gremlin queries.
A text blob containing the Gremlin Profile result. See Gremlin profile API in Neptune for details.

requestId.description: The unique identifier of the Gremlin query.

result.description: The Gremlin query output from the server.

code.description: The HTTP response code returned fro the Gremlin query request..

results.description: A text blob containing the openCypher explain results.

queryId.description: The ID of the query for which status is being returned.

queryEvalStats.description: The evaluation status of the Gremlin query.

waited.description: Indicates how long the query waited, in milliseconds.

elapsed.description: The number of milliseconds the query has been running so far.

cancelled.description: Set to TRUE if the query was cancelled, or FALSE otherwise.

subqueries.description: The number of subqueries in this query.

status.description: The HTTP response code for the request.

payload.description: Status information about the load job, in a layout that could look like this:

id.description: The unique identifier of this data-processing job.

failureReason.description: The failure reason, in case of a failure.

cloudwatchLogUrl.description: The CloudWatch log URL for the resource.

id.description: The unique identifier of the inference endpoint.

failureReason.description: The failure reason, in case of a failure.

cloudwatchLogUrl.description: The CloudWatch log URL for the resource.

status.description: The status of the model training job.

id.description: The unique identifier of this model-training job.

mlModels.description: A list of the configurations of the ML models being used.

id.description: The unique identifier of the model-transform job to be retrieved.

models.description: A list of the configuration information for the models being used.

queryId.description: The unique ID of the query for which status is being returned.

waited.description: Indicates how long the query waited, in milliseconds.

elapsed.description: The number of milliseconds the query has been running so far.

cancelled.description: Set to TRUE if the query was cancelled, or FALSE otherwise.

subqueries.description: The number of subqueries in this query.

status.description: The HTTP return code indicating the status of the load job.

payload.description: Contains a loadId name-value pair that provides an identifier for the load operation.

id.description: The unique ID of the new data processing job.

arn.description: The ARN of the data processing job.

creationTimeInMillis.description: The time it took to create the new processing job, in milliseconds.

id.description: The unique ID of the new model training job.

arn.description: The ARN of the new model training job.

creationTimeInMillis.description: The model training job creation time, in milliseconds.

id.description: The unique ID of the new model transform job.

arn.description: The ARN of the model transform job.

creationTimeInMillis.description: The creation time of the model transform job, in milliseconds.
